detail.plot: The misadventures of two modern-day Stone Age families, the Flintstones and the Rubbles.

episodes.title: The Flintstone Flyer
episodes.plot: Barney has invented something that looks to Fred like a giant egg beater, but it's a flying machine. Fred feigns illness so that he and Barney can get out of "opera night" with the girls and use the Flyer to go bowling instead.

episodes.title: Hot Lips Hannigan
episodes.plot: It's Fred's responsibility this time around to assemble an act for Amateur Night at his and Barney's club.

episodes.title: The Swimming Pool
episodes.plot: Fred is mad at Barney for calling him stupid. Then Barney decides to build a swimming pool, and Fred talks his neighbor into sharing the pool with the Flintstones.

episodes.title: No Help Wanted
episodes.plot: After causing Barney to get fired, Fred gets his friend a job as a "repo man." But Barney's first repossession case is at the Flintstones' house.

episodes.title: The Split Personality
episodes.plot: Fred gets hit on the head with a bottle, changing his personality into a sophisticated gentleman.

episodes.title: The Monster from the Tar Pits
episodes.plot: When movie-makers come to Bedrock, the girls are all excited, but it's Fred who lands a role in the film.

episodes.title: The Babysitters
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney reluctantly agree to babysit a neighbor's baby.

episodes.title: At the Races
episodes.plot: Fred wants to buy his friend's pool hall. He intends to finance the purchase by betting a week's paycheck at the dinosaur races.

episodes.title: The Engagement Ring
episodes.plot: In order to get BACK the money, Fred has Barney do boxing.

episodes.title: Hollyrock, Here I Come
episodes.plot: Wilma and Betty win an all-expenses-paid trip to Hollyrock, which gets even better when Wilma is offered a part in a show.

episodes.title: The Golf Champion
episodes.plot: When Barney, in his official capacity, cites Fred for violation of the rules and strips Fred of a club championship he won, Fred declares war on his old friend.

episodes.title: The Sweepstakes Ticket
episodes.plot: Fred buys a sweepstakes ticket and decides to share it with Barney and give it to him for safekeeping; then Fred decides he can't trust his old pal.

episodes.title: The Drive - In
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney buy a drive-in without telling Wilma and Betty.

episodes.title: The Prowler
episodes.plot: A burglar is on the loose in Bedrock. Betty and Wilma take judo classes from Mr Hashimoto, and Fred decides to play a trick on Wilma by disguising himself as a prowler.

episodes.title: The Girls' Night Out
episodes.plot: Fred records a record in an amusement-park booth and by mistake leaves it behind. When the record is found and played on the airwaves, it becomes a big hit, and Fred's a star.

episodes.title: Arthur Quarry's Dance Class
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney are sneaking out at night--to take dance lessons, so they won't embarrass the girls at an upcoming ball.

episodes.title: The Big Bank Robbery
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney are mistaken for the bank robbers when they find the bag of stolen money.

episodes.title: The Snorkasaurus Hunter
episodes.plot: To save money on meat, Fred decides to turn his vacation into a hunting trip, where the Flintstones and Rubbles meet up with a talking dinosaur.

episodes.title: The Hot Piano
episodes.plot: Fred would love to give Wilma a real stunner of an anniversary gift--a baby grand piano--but its cost is way out of his league, until "Eighty-Eight Fingers Louie" offers him a real steal of a deal.

episodes.title: The Hypnotist
episodes.plot: Barney is hypnotized into thinking he is a dog.

episodes.title: Love Letters on the Rocks
episodes.plot: Fred finds old love letters to Wilma, and, failing to recognize them as his own, he hires a detective to track down Wilma's "secret lover."

episodes.title: The Tycoon
episodes.plot: Fred is hired to impersonate a rich business man look-alike temporarily, leading to confusion and friction from his wife and the Rubbles.

episodes.title: The Astra' Nuts
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney go to a recruitment center by mistake and find themselves signed up for three years of military service.

episodes.title: The Long, Long Weekend
episodes.plot: The Flintstones and the Rubbles are invited to a free vacation at a beach hotel by Fred's old friend the owner. Unaware there is a motive for the generosity.

episodes.title: In the Dough
episodes.plot: Wilma's "Flint-Rubble-Double-Bubble Cake" wins a baking contest, but when her sudden illness keeps her from competing at the next level, Fred decides to disguise himself and take his wife's place.

episodes.title: The Good Scout
episodes.plot: Barney and Fred forsake bowling to become leaders of scout troops.

episodes.title: Rooms for Rent
episodes.plot: The Flintstones and the Rubbles rent a spare room in their home to earn extra money, but Fred and Barney aren't happy with their new boarders.

episodes.title: Fred Flintstone: Before and After
episodes.plot: Used without his consent as the "before" picture in a weight-loss pitch, Fred decides to accept the challenge to lose thirty pounds in thirty days when told of the money that can be his.

episodes.title: The Hit Song Writers
episodes.plot: Fred's latest get-rich-quick scheme is songwriting. He enlists the help of Hoagy Carmichael.

episodes.title: Droop Along Flintstone
episodes.plot: The Flintstones and the Rubbles stay at Fred's rich cousin's ranch while they are away.

episodes.title: The Missing Bus
episodes.plot: Dissatisfied at work, Fred quits the gravel pit and is soon hired to be a school-bus driver. On the first day, he realizes he might have made a mistake.

episodes.title: Alvin Brickrock Presents
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney have been eavesdropping on a new, mysterious neighbor and his wife. When the man brings over a trunk and asks Fred to keep it for him, Fred suspects the man's wife's body may be inside.

episodes.title: Fred Flintstone Woos Again
episodes.plot: Fred reluctantly takes Wilma on a second honeymoon, but soon discovers that the official who conducted their wedding was never licensed. As a result, Wilma attempts to get Fred to woo her once again.

episodes.title: The Rock Quarry Story
episodes.plot: A movie star tired of his life as an actor, decides to hide out in Bedrock, where he befriends Fred and Barney.

episodes.title: The Soft Touchables
episodes.plot: Moonlighting private detectives Fred and Barney are misled by a sexy prospect into a dangerous scheme.

episodes.title: Flintstone of Prinstone
episodes.plot: Hoping to advance at the quarry, Fred enrolls in the "Prinstone University" night class in accounting. He finds the classwork tough and even falls asleep on the job. Meanwhile, Mr. Slate wants Fred to join the quarry's football team, not realizing that football interfered with Fred's studies in his youth.

episodes.title: The Little White Lie
episodes.plot: Fred doesn't want Wilma to know that he lied to play poker, so he pretends that his winnings came from a lost wallet--and honest Wilma places an ad for the wallet's "owner."

episodes.title: Social Climbers
episodes.plot: Wilma and Betty want to attend the socialites' ball thrown by an old friend who married money, but Fred and Barney expect they'll have a lot more fun at the down-to-earth Firemen's Ball.

episodes.title: The Beauty Contest
episodes.plot: The Loyal Order of Water Buffaloes organizes a beauty contest, and Fred and Barney are chosen to judge it, against their will, as they anticipate Wilma and Betty's jealousy. They must deal with the girls' displeasure as a parade of contestants come to their houses, and with pressure from various contestants' boosters, including Mr. Slate, who wants his daughter to be chosen, and a gangster who wants them to pick his "Cookie."

episodes.title: The Masquerade Ball
episodes.plot: The Flintstones and the Rubbles attend a charity masquerade ball to mingle with high society.

episodes.title: The Picnic
episodes.plot: Fred thinks the key to winning the competition at the annual picnic is to team with Joe Rockhead instead of best pal Barney.

episodes.title: The House Guest
episodes.plot: When the Rubble 's home is flooded. Fred invites his neighbors to stay over his house temporary but Wilma is against the idea thinking Fred and Barney will start bickering.

episodes.title: The X-Ray Story
episodes.plot: Barney and the girls fight to keep Fred awake for seventy-two hours without telling him why: they believe that if they let him fall asleep during that time, he'll die.

episodes.title: The Gambler
episodes.plot: Fred's old gambling habit has been reawakened.

episodes.title: A Star Is Almost Born
episodes.plot: Wilma meets a TV producer who wants to use her in his next commercial.

episodes.title: The Entertainer
episodes.plot: Fred needs to entertain a comely client for work, but decides to skip the explanation to Wilma.

episodes.title: Wilma's Vanishing Money
episodes.plot: Wilma has hidden cash to purchase a gift for Fred: a bowling ball. Fred finds the cash and, believing the money itself to be his gift, takes it and goes to buy the bowling ball. However, back at home, Wilma believes she has been robbed and reports the disappearance of the money to the police. Feeling guilty, Fred wants to return the bowling ball and re-stash the money. Meanwhile, Arnold the newsboy smells something fishy.

episodes.title: Feudin' and Fussin'
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney's latest spat results in Barney's decision to sell his house. A rich Texan is very interested in the property, and Fred must come up with a scheme to keep his old buddy as his neighbor.

episodes.title: Impractical Joker
episodes.plot: Barney's plan to put a stop to Fred's practical jokes may backfire.

episodes.title: Operation Barney
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney call in sick to their bosses in order to see a baseball game on a work day.

episodes.title: The Happy Household
episodes.plot: Wilma's new cooking show on television is a hit with everyone except Fred, who's anything but a "happy pappy" as he's forced to go without Wilma's home-cooked meals.

episodes.title: Fred Strikes Out
episodes.plot: There is a championship tournament at Bedrock Bowl and Fred must compete in it, but Wilma wants him to take her out that night. So Fred takes Wilma to a drive-in movie next door to the bowling alley. He manages to sneak in a bowl and return to her quickly. However, Wilma accidentally hurts Fred's thumb and Barney sticks the bowling ball atop Fred's swelling digit, which Fred then can't get out of the ball. Will he be able to get out of his lie to Wilma?

episodes.title: This Is Your Lifesaver
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney save a guy named J. Montague Gypsum from throwing himself off the George Washingstone Bridge. In return, Gypsum offers to be their servant for life. But Gypsum is a con man who tries to take over the Flintstone household. Fred tries to come up with a plan whereby Gypsum can save Fred's life so that they can then be even and Gypsum can be sent on his way.

episodes.title: Trouble-In-Law
episodes.plot: Fred finds a suitor for his live-in mother-in-law who may be a notorious con man.

episodes.title: The Mailman Cometh
episodes.plot: Fred regrets sending Mr. Slate a nasty letter.

episodes.title: The Rock Vegas Story
episodes.plot: A trip to Rock Vegas reawakens Fred's itch to gamble.

episodes.title: Divided We Sail
episodes.plot: Barney goes on a tv game show on Fred's ticket and wins a house boat.

episodes.title: Kleptomaniac Caper
episodes.plot: Wilma intends to donate Fred's old clothes to a rummage sale, but puts the box in Barney's car, leading Fred to believe that Barney stole his clothes and is a kleptomaniac.

episodes.title: Latin Lover
episodes.plot: Annoyed that Wilma has a crush on Italian actor Roberto Rockellini, Fred tries to win her back by growing a mustache, wearing an ascot, and being charming--and it may be working; now Wilma's getting jealous.

episodes.title: Take Me Out to the Ball Game
episodes.plot: Fred becomes a Little League umpire, but is unprepared for the intensity of the competition among the kids' parents.

episodes.title: Dino Goes Hollyrock
episodes.plot: A crush on a lady dinosaurus who's the star of her own television show may lead to TV stardom for Dino--and to the loss of their pet for the Flintstones.

episodes.title: Fred's New Boss
episodes.plot: When Barney loses his job, he prevails upon Fred to try to get him hired on at the gravel pit. The introduction of Fred's neighbor to his boss results in the startling discovery that Barney and Mr. Slate are relatives--and to an instant executive suite for Barney. All at once, Fred's old buddy is his new boss.

episodes.title: Barney the Invisible
episodes.plot: Fred's latest "get-rich-quick" scheme is to invent a new soft drink. His concoction may not have what it takes to displace the beverages dominating the market, but it makes Barney invisible.

episodes.title: Bowling Ballet
episodes.plot: Fred enrols in ballet school to help him get his bowling form back for a big bowling tournament.

episodes.title: The Twitch
episodes.plot: Against the odds, Fred lands a star attraction for Wilma's fund-raiser, but an allergic reaction to pickled dodo eggs may prevent him from appearing.

episodes.title: Here's Snow in Your Eyes
episodes.plot: During a lodge convention at a ski resort, jewel thieves mistake Barney for their chief conspirator.

episodes.title: The Buffalo Convention
episodes.plot: Fred's birthday gift to Wilma is a bird that talks--and may spill the beans about the boys' planned trip to Frantic City for a Water Buffalo convention.

episodes.title: The Little Stranger
episodes.plot: Fred gets excited when he overhears Wilma talking about a new addition to the family.

episodes.title: Baby Barney
episodes.plot: Fred's rich uncle has no heir, so Fred drafts Barney into posing as his son so that his uncle will leave his estate to Fred.

episodes.title: Hawaiian Escapade
episodes.plot: In order to win a trip to visit her idol on the set of his show, Wilma submits a contest entry that depicts Fred as a superhero of sorts.

episodes.title: Ladies' Day
episodes.plot: Fred wants to go to the big game, and if it means dressing up as a woman to get in, so be it.

episodes.title: Nuttin' But the Tooth
episodes.plot: Barney has a severe aching tooth that needs to be extracted. But Fred wants to avoid the dental bill by trying to pull the tooth loose himself. Then, Fred took Barney to a veterinarian office for ill pets (like Dino). Just as the veterinarian starts to inject gas into Barney, he is interrupted leaving Barney alone. Barney falls into a deep sleep from the additional gas and became airborne. Fortunately, Fred saw Barney float by and away. Fred quickly found Barney outside and floating in the air. After Fred lassos Barney and pulls him to the ground. Fred's quick jerk also removed Barney's aching tooth. With the additional gas inside Barney & knowing how long the gas will last, they get to see the boxing match & free of charge. Fred ties two ropes on to Barney, one at his feet and one at his shoulders, making Barney Rubble a temporary "Stone-Age hot-air blimp".

episodes.title: High School Fred
episodes.plot: Fred's embarrassed that he lacks a high-school diploma and has returned to classes with teenagers in order to keep his job.

episodes.title: Dial S for Suspicion
episodes.plot: Fred suspects dear Wilma of having designs on his life for the insurance money.

episodes.title: Flash Gun Freddie
episodes.plot: Fred's new "Polarock" camera is an occasion for all kinds of misadventure for him and Barney.

episodes.title: The Kissing Burglar
episodes.plot: Fred wants to teach Wilma a lesson for hoping the kissing burglar who's active in the neighborhood will come to their house.

episodes.title: Wilma, the Maid
episodes.plot: The Flintstones new maid abruptly quits on the day Fred invites Mr. Slate to dinner.

episodes.title: The Hero
episodes.plot: Barney saves a baby's life, but Fred takes the credit.

episodes.title: The Surprise
episodes.plot: Because he and Betty are babysitting a nephew, Barney hasn't got time to go to a bowling tournament with Fred, who therefore strongly dislikes the baby.

episodes.title: Mother-In-Law's Visit
episodes.plot: Wilma's mother visits and father-to-be Fred wants to show he's a kind man by being nice to her, even tough she's very hostile to him.

episodes.title: Foxy Grandma
episodes.plot: Fred tries to find a maid so that Wilma doesn't have to do household chores. This turns out to be easier said than done.

episodes.title: Fred's New Job
episodes.plot: Fred wants to ask his boss for a raise and Barney has an idea to get it. However, instead of getting a raise, Fred gets fired.

episodes.title: The Blessed Event (The Dress Rehearsal)
episodes.plot: According to the doctor, any day now, the blessed event can take place. Fred and Barney undertake a rehearsal.

episodes.title: Carry On, Nurse Fred
episodes.plot: Wilma's mother has sent a very strict and rigid nurse to take care of baby Pebbles and the household.

episodes.title: Ventriloquist Barney
episodes.plot: Barney asks Fred to go to a wrestling game with him. Fred wants to go, but he promised Wilma to babysit Pebbles.

episodes.title: The Big Move
episodes.plot: Thinking Barney is a bad influence on Pebbles, Fred decides the Flintstones should move to a more elite neighborhood.

episodes.title: Swedish Visitors
episodes.plot: Wilma rents out the house to Swedish musicians while the Flintstones meet Yogi Bear at a national park.

episodes.title: The Birthday Party
episodes.plot: Because she needs to prepare for the surprise party for Fred's birthday, Wilma asks Barney to drive Fred around all day. Things don't exactly go as planned after the two of them fall asleep.

episodes.title: Ann-Margrock Presents
episodes.plot: Brought by happenstance to Fred's house and unrecognized by the boys, star Ann-Margrock helps them prepare for an appearance at the Bedrock Bowl.

episodes.title: Groom Gloom
episodes.plot: Fred dreams he's getting obnoxious Arnold the newsboy as a son-in-law.

episodes.title: Little Bamm-Bamm
episodes.plot: After the childless Rubbles wish upon a star for a baby of their own, Barney finds a basket containing the world's strongest foundling. However, someone else wants to adopt little Bamm-Bamm, and he's rich.

episodes.title: Dino Disappears
episodes.plot: When Fred mistakenly accuses Dino of trying to hurt Pebbles, the family's beloved pet runs away.

episodes.title: Fred's Monkeyshines
episodes.plot: Glasses with the wrong prescription cause Fred to mistake a monkey for his Pebbles.

episodes.title: The Flintstone Canaries
episodes.plot: The barbershop quartet Fred organizes to advertise "Soft Soap" is a bathtub quartet, because Barney can carry a tune only when he's in the tub.

episodes.title: Glue for Two
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney both get glued to Barney's new bowling ball.

episodes.title: Big League Freddie
episodes.plot: Major-league scouts witness a star-quality outing at a company baseball game and want to sign Fred, not realizing that only the uniform was Flintstone's.

episodes.title: Old Lady Betty
episodes.plot: Betty gets a little job to buy Barney a gift, and her work is simple and puzzling: use big bills to make small purchases.

episodes.title: Sleep On, Sweet Fred
episodes.plot: Wilma and Betty use a sleep teaching technique on Fred and Barney to get anything they want.

episodes.title: Kleptomaniac Pebbles
episodes.plot: Fred takes Pebbles to the supermarket and discovers she puts a lot of things in the basket. Fred is afraid his daughter might be a budding kleptomaniac. While Fred's at the jeweler's to purchase a gift for Wilma, a thief plants an expensive diamond collar in Pebbles's crib. Back home, Wilma is delighted with the "gift," but Fred suspects that Pebbles snatched it. He goes back at night with Barney to the jewelry store to sneak the collar back, but the thief is waiting there to recover his loot.

episodes.title: Daddy's Little Beauty
episodes.plot: When Fred hears of a "most beautiful baby" contest, he of course enters Pebbles.

episodes.title: Daddies Anonymous
episodes.plot: Fred's beginning to think that taking Pebbles for walks in her stroller is bad for his image. Then he hears of a club in which fathers can park their babies' carriages while they go attend to other things.

episodes.title: Peek-a-Boo Camera
episodes.plot: The Flintstones and the Rubbles are fans of "Peek-a-boo Camera," a show that catches people off their guard as they go about daily life. Fred and Barney are invited to a bachelor party thrown by the Water Buffaloes. The men lie to their wives that they are going to visit a brother Buffalo dying in the hospital as well as attend his funeral. Fred and Barney attend the bachelor party instead. They are unwittingly filmed by the crew of "Peek-a-boo Camera".

episodes.title: Once Upon a Coward
episodes.plot: Fred complies with an armed robber's demand that he offer no resistance, but sweats thereafter with the fear that he has been a coward.

episodes.title: Ten Little Flintstones
episodes.plot: An alien spacecraft lands in Bedrock and creates ten clones of Fred who cause him trouble.

episodes.title: Fred el Terrifico
episodes.plot: On vacation in Rockapulco, Fred hams it up as "El Terrifico," and some jewel smugglers decide to take advantage of his innocent pose for their own gain.

episodes.title: The Bedrock Hillbillies
episodes.plot: Fred inherits an estate, when he goes to claim it reignites a long time feud with a hillbilly family.

episodes.title: Flintstone and the Lion
episodes.plot: Fred finds a cute kitten and brings it home. The little feline has an enormous appetite, and in time it becomes clear that this is no house cat, but a lion. Attached to his pet, Fred avoids facing the reality of what he's brought under his roof as long as he can.

episodes.title: Cave Scout Jamboree
episodes.plot: The two families' vacation to a famous campground coincides with a huge, international scout jamboree.

episodes.title: Room for Two
episodes.plot: The latest feud between Flintstone and Rubble erupts when Barney votes for Joe Rockhead instead of Fred to be the lodge's next Grand Poobah. Fred tells Barney they're through. Then Barney learns that the new room Fred built extends onto Barney's side of the property line. Now Fred must endure in his own house the presence of his new enemy.

episodes.title: Ladies' Night at the Lodge
episodes.plot: Wilma and Betty seal into Fred and Barney's Water Buffalo Lodge disguised as men.

episodes.title: Reel Trouble
episodes.plot: Fred annoys everybody by constantly showing home videos he made of Pebbles.

episodes.title: Son of Rockzilla
episodes.plot: To promote their new movie, "Son of Rockzilla," the producers hire Fred to wear Rockzilla's costume. However, Fred is unable to remove the creature's frightful mask. Now he is being pursued by both cops and an infatuated female Rockzilla-like creature who's broken out of the zoo to follow her new love.

episodes.title: Bachelor Daze
episodes.plot: Fred, Wilma, Barney and Betty reminisce about the first time they met.

episodes.title: Operation Switchover
episodes.plot: Fred and Wilma wager whose job is harder by swapping jobs for a day.

episodes.title: Hop Happy
episodes.plot: The Rubbles' new kangaroo-like pet is annoying to Fred until the animal proves its worth at a picnic.

episodes.title: Monster Fred
episodes.plot: A bowling ball falls on Fred's head and Barney takes him to a doctor. However, this Dr. Frankenstone has a machine that can switch personalities and he's looking for a human guinea pig.

episodes.title: Itty Biddy Freddy
episodes.plot: Fred invented a fat-reducing drink. That is, he thinks he did, but after he drinks it, he shrinks and ends up being about a foot high.

episodes.title: Pebbles' Birthday Party
episodes.plot: Fred has to organize two parties and one hour apart. Pebbles' first birthday party, he already planned. Plus, he and Barney were picked by the Grand Poobah to create a Water Buffalo Stag party, because they arrived late.

episodes.title: Bedrock Rodeo Round-Up
episodes.plot: With Wilma's old boyfriend impressing her and Pebbles with his rodeo tricks, Fred decides to enter the rodeo himself.

episodes.title: Cinderellastone
episodes.plot: Fred is stung by the slight of being the only employee not invited to Mr. Slate's party. But it seems he may get a chance to attend after all, in the guise of a mysterious, suave, exotic stranger.

episodes.title: A Haunted House Is Not a Home
episodes.plot: Fred stands to inherit a mansion, if he can pass a night there--and survive multiple attempts on his life.

episodes.title: Dr. Sinister
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney are caught up in a swirl of spies' intrigue, with exotic and menacing strangers and multiple threats on their lives, all while Wilma and Betty are waiting for them to return with the burgers and buns.

episodes.title: The Gruesomes
episodes.plot: A very sinister-looking but affable couple and their child move into a mansion next door to the Flintstones.

episodes.title: The Most Beautiful Baby in Bedrock
episodes.plot: While their parents argue about which couple has the more beautiful child, Pebbles and Bamm-Bamm take to their knees and crawl away.

episodes.title: Dino and Juliet
episodes.plot: While Fred and his obnoxious new neighbor argue, Dino and the neighbor's lady dinosaurus fall in love.

episodes.title: King for a Night
episodes.plot: Fred is hired to impersonate a look alike king for one evening, while the real king flees.

episodes.title: Indianrockolis 500
episodes.plot: Fred hopes to win an auto race to pay for the kids' higher education, but he's having all sorts of problems with the racing car that Barney built.

episodes.title: Adobe Dick
episodes.plot: On a fishing trip, Fred and Barney find themselves in the belly of a whale.

episodes.title: Christmas Flintstone
episodes.plot: Fred is such a natural as a department store's Santa that news of him reaches the North Pole, where Kris Kringle has him in mind for much more important work on Christmas Eve.

episodes.title: Fred's Flying Lesson
episodes.plot: A free flying lesson from a sexy lady instructor persuades Fred to go for his pilot's license.

episodes.title: Fred's Second Car
episodes.plot: Fred buys a used car at a police auction and pursued by gangsters who want the car.

episodes.title: Time Machine
episodes.plot: Not everyone a caveman encounters in the future will be helpful, sympathetic, or even patient. Some will be downright menacing.

episodes.title: The Hatrocks and the Gruesomes
episodes.plot: The Flintstones need to get rid of their house guests, the hillbilly Hatrocks, and hope that their sinister neighbors, the Gruesomes, can be of help.

episodes.title: Moonlight and Maintenance
episodes.plot: Fred and the family move into an apartment where Fred takes a second job as a superintendent.

episodes.title: Sheriff for a Day
episodes.plot: The sheriff of an untamed western town offers visiting Fred his badge for a day, but doesn't tell him that three outlaws are on their way to the town to eliminate the sheriff, no matter who he is.

episodes.title: Deep in the Heart of Texarock
episodes.plot: On vacation on a ranch in Texarock, the Flintstones and the Rubbles are menaced by cattle rustlers.

episodes.title: The Rolls Rock Caper
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney are recruited on the street by a private detective.

episodes.title: Superstone
episodes.plot: A TV superhero quits without notice, and Fred is drafted from the audience to take his place.

episodes.title: Fred Meets Hercurock
episodes.plot: Fred is cast as "Hercurock" in a new film; he finds the life of a star, and Wilma the life of a star's wife, not to be all that they thought.

episodes.title: Surfin' Fred
episodes.plot: On vacation at the beach, Fred is a hit with the surfing teenagers, but not with the lifeguard, who must repeatedly rescue him.

episodes.title: No Biz Like Show Biz
episodes.plot: Fred dreams that Pebbles and Bam - Bam become sensational singing stars.

episodes.title: The House That Fred Built
episodes.plot: Fred buys and fixes up a run down house for his mother-in-law to live.

episodes.title: The Return of Stony Curtis
episodes.plot: Stoney Curtis is in town shooting a film. To promote it, a contest offers the prize of Stoney as slave for a day. Wilma and Betty win, and Fred sets Stoney to work as if he really were a slave. At Wilma's prompting, Stoney encourages Fred to believe that he himself could become a Hollyrock star, and Fred falls for the bait so seriously that he decides to put his house up for sale.

episodes.title: Disorder in the Court
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney are selected for jury duty. Fred is elected as jury foreman.

episodes.title: Circus Business
episodes.plot: Fred invests his income-tax refund in the purchase of an ailing circus. The performers, whom the former owner left unpaid, walk out on him, so Fred and Barney try to put on a show themselves.

episodes.title: Samantha
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney go on a weekend camping trip, claiming that women can't rough it as they do. In response, Samantha Stephens takes Wilma, Betty, and the children camping, using her magical twitch of the nose whenever necessary to upstage the boys.

episodes.title: The Great Gazoo
episodes.plot: Fred and Barney meet The Great Gazoo, a little green alien sent to Earth to grant them magical wishes.

episodes.title: Rip Van Flintstone
episodes.plot: Fred falls asleep at the company picnic and dreams he has awaken 20 years later.

episodes.title: The Gravelberry Pie King
episodes.plot: Fred gets fired from his job and goes into business making and selling pies.

episodes.title: The Stonefinger Caper
episodes.plot: Barney is mistaken for for a scientist and pursued by spies.

episodes.title: The Masquerade Party
episodes.plot: The Flintstones and the Rubbles attend a costume party at the Water Buffalo Lodge on the same night as a radio report as Bedrock is " invaded" by outer space aliens The Way outs.

episodes.title: Shinrock-A-Go-Go
episodes.plot: Fred invents a new dance craze after a bowling ball drops on his foot.

episodes.title: Royal Rubble
episodes.plot: Rockarabians seek the missing heir to their nation's throne. They know what he looks like: enough like Barney Rubble to be his twin. Ignoring his protests that they have the wrong fellow, the Rockarabians carry Barney bodily to the hotel at which their delegation is staying. Barney begins to think it might not be so bad to become an exotic monarch, until he is told he will have to select many beauties for his harem--something he knows Betty will never allow.

episodes.title: Seeing Doubles
episodes.plot: Betty and Wilma want to go out for the evening, while the boys want to practice for an upcoming bowling tournament. No problem: the Great Gazoo creates doubles of Barney and Fred. Or, more precisely, the doubles are the beginning of a big problem.

episodes.title: How to Pick a Fight with Your Wife Without Really Trying
episodes.plot: Spats between spouses escalate to such a degree that the couples split: Fred and Barney are thrown together as roommates, while Wilma and Betty are bunking together at the other house.

episodes.title: Fred Goes Ape
episodes.plot: Fred has violent sneezing caused by an allergy. To stop the sneezing, he goes to the drug store for a bottle of "Scram." Each such pill stops a fit of sneezing, but has the side effect of turning Fred temporarily into an ape.

episodes.title: The Long, Long, Long Weekend
episodes.plot: Gazoo takes the Flintstones and the Rubbles into the 21st Century.

episodes.title: Two Men on a Dinosaur
episodes.plot: At the races at Hollyrock Park, Fred and Barney wager on dinosaurs picked by the Great Gazoo. Gangsters take note of their success and kidnap the two friends so that they can make winning picks for them.

episodes.title: The Treasure of Sierra Madrock
episodes.plot: Traveling through the desert on their way to Rock Vegas, Fred and Barney fall prey to con men who have seeded the river with phony gold. When it turns out that the river contains real gold after all, the con men will do whatever it takes to oust the Flintstones and Rubbles from their claim.

episodes.title: Curtain Call at Bedrock
episodes.plot: For Wilma's charity production, Fred and Barney are somehow cast as Shakespeare's famous star-crossed lovers.

episodes.title: Boss for a Day
episodes.plot: Gazoo allows Fred to run the quarry for a day to see what life is like as The Boss.

episodes.title: Fred's Island
episodes.plot: Mr. Slate gives Fred the use of his yacht--on condition that Fred paint it. Fred falls asleep and wakes to find himself aground on a desert island. Or is it an amusement park?

episodes.title: Jealousy
episodes.plot: When Fred chooses bowling over taking her to a ball, Wilma calls upon a rich, dashing old boyfriend to escort her to the ball instead. Fred ultimately decides to crash the ball later in the evening, escorting his own date--Barney, transformed into "Barbara" by the Great Gazoo.

episodes.title: Dripper
episodes.plot: The cavemen and their children meet "Dripper," a dolphin-like sealasaurus and star of a show at a marine park. Dripper is the target of an abduction plot. Thinking they have Dripper, the kidnappers instead abduct Barney, who's dressed in scuba gear.

episodes.title: My Fair Freddy
episodes.plot: When the Flintstones are invited to a posh ball Gazoo gives Fred ballet lessons to be more cultured.

episodes.title: The Story of Rocky's Raiders
episodes.plot: Fred reads from his grandfather's diary of his exploits as hero of the First Rock War.
